report_name: INTERPOL: Cybercrime Surges in Asia-Pacific, Accounting for 30% of All Crime in Some Nations

threat_actors.name: Southeast Asian cybercriminal groups
threat_actors.description: Organized criminal networks in Southeast Asia identified by INTERPOL as increasingly discussing the use of AI and deepfakes for malicious activities. These groups are driving a surge in cybercrime, including phishing and ransomware, to an industrial scale in the Asia-Pacific region.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through widespread online scams, phishing, and ransomware attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Identified as the most prevalent and financially damaging threat in the Asia-Pacific region, with individuals in the region clicking malicious links at twice the global average. Cloud applications are the most common targets.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Used in over 135,000 ransomware attacks in the Asia-Pacific region in 2024, affecting sectors like real estate, manufacturing, and financial services. Ransomware was present in 51% of all system intrusion incidents.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: The use of deepfakes and other AI tools for malicious purposes is a growing concern, with a 600% increase in discussions about their use on cybercriminal forums between February and June 2024.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: Leveraged by cybercriminals in the Asia-Pacific region to power sophisticated scams. INTERPOL observed a 600% increase in discussions on cybercriminal forums about using AI and deepfakes for malicious purposes.

identities.name: Real Estate Sector
identities.description: Identified by INTERPOL as one of the most affected sectors by ransomware attacks in the Asia-Pacific region in 2024.

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: Identified by INTERPOL as one of the most affected sectors by ransomware attacks in the Asia-Pacific region in 2024.

identities.name: Financial Services Sector
identities.description: Identified by INTERPOL as one of the most affected sectors by ransomware attacks in the Asia-Pacific region in 2024.

identities.name: Cloud Applications
identities.description: The most common targets for phishing campaigns in the Asia-Pacific region, according to the INTERPOL report.

infrastructure.name: Cybercriminal Forums and Telegram Channels
infrastructure.description: Platforms used by Southeast Asian cybercriminal groups to discuss the use of AI and deepfakes, with a 600% increase in such discussions observed by INTERPOL.

indicators.name: Generic Phishing Email Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Generic Phishing Email Detection
id: 5b9e1a2f-3c4d-4e5f-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects emails containing common phishing lures and a URL, indicative of phishing attempts as highlighted as a major threat in the INTERPOL 2025/2026 Asia and South Pacific Cyberthreat Assessment Report.
references:
  - https://www.interpol.int/News-and-Events/News/2026/New-INTERPOL-report-highlights-escalating-cyber-threats-across-Asia-and-South-Pacific
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection_keywords:
    Subject|contains:
      - 'Urgent Action Required'
      - 'Account Suspension'
      - 'Security Alert'
      - 'Unusual Sign-in'
      - 'Verify Your Account'
    Body|contains:
      - 'click here'
      - 'log in to'
      - 'update your details'
  selection_link:
    Body|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate password reset or account notification emails.
indicators.description: Detects emails with common phishing keywords and embedded links, reflecting the prevalent phishing threat described in the INTERPOL report.
